prompt.overview: This prompt defines a full QA workflow including: (1) Generate additional test cases (2) Generate ~100 seed data records (3) Execute all tests (old + new) (4) Regression reporting

phase.name: Generate Additional Test Cases
phase.instruction: Generate at least 10 new test cases following the given XML structure. Cover modules: User, Article, Comment, Reaction, Bookmark, Notification, Category, Tag. Include: - Positive cases - Negative cases - Boundary cases - Pagination cases (IMPORTANT) - Sorting/filtering cases where applicable
phase.pagination_focus: Ensure test cases include: - page, limit parameters - total, totalPages validation - empty page handling - invalid pagination inputs - sorting + pagination combined

case.description: Get articles with pagination and sorting by views desc
case.preconditions: At least 50 articles exist with varying views
case.expected_result: 200 OK, top 10 articles with highest views returned in descending order

case.description: Filter articles by status with pagination
case.preconditions: Multiple articles exist with different statuses
case.expected_result: 200 OK, only published articles returned with correct pagination
200 OK, empty data array with correct pagination metadata

case.description: Get comments of an article with pagination
Get nested replies for a comment
case.expected_result: 200 OK, returns only replies of the parent comment

case.preconditions: Article has multiple reactions of different types
case.expected_result: 200 OK, returns count grouped by LIKE, LOVE, LAUGH

case.description: User reacts twice to same article
case.preconditions: User already reacted to the article
User has bookmarked at least 20 articles
User has both read and unread notifications

case.description: Mark notifications as read and verify state change
case.preconditions: At least one unread notification exists

case.expected_result: 200 OK, only articles with the given tag returned

case.description: Search articles by keyword with pagination
case.expected_result: 200 OK, matching articles returned with pagination

case.description: Retrieve version history of an article
case.expected_result: 200 OK, list of versions in chronological order

case.description: Switch reaction type for same article
case.expected_result: Reaction updated from LIKE to LOVE
400 Bad Request or foreign key violation

phase.name: Generate Test Data (~100 records)
phase.instruction: Generate approximately 100 records across all entities while ensuring referential integrity.
phase.distribution: Users: 10-15 records Categories: 10-15 records (hierarchical) Articles: 30-40 records Tags: 10-20 records Comments: 20-30 records (including nested) Reactions: 20-30 records Bookmarks: 15-25 records Notifications: generated from interactions
phase.rules: - All foreign keys must be valid - Use realistic IDs - Ensure variety in: - Article status (DRAFT, PUBLISHED, ARCHIVED) - Reaction types (LIKE, LOVE, LAUGH) - User roles (AUTHOR, EDITOR, VIEWER) - Include edge scenarios: - Articles without tags - Users with no activity - Articles with high interaction counts

phase.name: Execute Full Test Suite
phase.instruction: Execute all test cases (existing + newly generated) against the seeded database.
phase.steps: 1. Initialize database with generated seed data 2. Run each test case sequentially 3. Validate: - API response status - Response payload - Database state changes - Constraints (unique, foreign keys) 4. Capture actual vs expected results
phase.validation_criteria: - HTTP status correctness - Data integrity - Pagination correctness (data length, metadata) - Sorting/filtering correctness - Constraint violations handling

phase.name: Regression Summary & Reporting
phase.instruction: Summarize execution results of all test cases.
phase.report_requirements: - Total test cases executed - Passed / Failed counts - Failure categorization by module - Root cause analysis - Performance or data issues (if any)
phase.output_format: - Summary table - List of failed cases with details - Recommendations for fixes
